title: 10 best herbal creams for constipation

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cassia creams helps with constipation because they contain senna, a natural laxative that stimulates bowel movements and softens stool. The cream's active ingredients, such as glycosides and anthraquinones, help to increase the frequency and ease of defecation, reducing symptoms like bloating and discomfort. Additionally, cassia creams can also soothe and calm the digestive tract, making it an effective remedy for constipation sufferers seeking a natural and gentle solution.
remedy_properties: Senna glycosides: Senna glycosides, specifically sennoside A and B, have a laxative effect by stimulating bowel movements and increasing gut motility, helping to relieve constipation.

Tannins: Tannins in cassia cream help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to chronic constipation, and also aid in improving stool texture and frequency.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in cassia cream have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and protect the gut lining, reducing oxidative stress and promoting a healthy gut microbiome, both of which can contribute to improved bowel function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Cassia roots are rich in sennosides, which stimulate bowel movements and help relieve constipation.
Rhyzomes: Cassia rhyzomes contain cinnamic acid, a natural stimulant that helps regulate digestive function and ease constipation symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh senna alexandrina leaves and flowers at dawn for maximum potency.
Dry the plant material in a warm, dark place for two weeks to preserve bioactivity.
Combine one part dried senna alexandrina leaves and flowers with three parts coconut oil and infuse for six hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard solids before adding beeswax and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 1:1.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 10 minutes to create a smooth, consistent cream that can be stored in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with constipation because of its natural ability to stimulate digestive processes. The herbs contained in these creams, such as dandelion root and leaf, have been traditionally used to support liver function and increase bile production, which aids in the breakdown of fats and the absorption of nutrients. Additionally, dandelion's natural laxative properties help to soften stool and promote regular bowel movements, effectively alleviating constipation symptoms. This natural approach can provide relief from discomfort and restore a healthy digestive rhythm.
remedy_properties: Laxative action: Dandelion root extract has been shown to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation due to its ability to increase muscle contractions in the intestine and improve gut motility.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and chronic constipation.

Digestive enzyme stimulant: The flavonoids present in dandelion flowers have been found to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, including bile and pancreatic juices, which aid in breaking down nutrients and promoting regular bowel movements.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps regulate bowel movements and relieve symptoms of constipation.
Barks: Containing triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion leaves and flowers measuring 2 cups.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried dandelion root with 2 cups boiling water steeping for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl retaining 1 cup of liquid discard solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler heating until melted.
Combine the herbal liquid with the melted wax-oil mixture stirring well before pouring into containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with constipation because of its natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and cramps, which often accompany constipation. Additionally, ginger's warming effect on the digestive tract helps to relax the muscles and improve stool movement, making it easier to pass stools and alleviate discomfort associated with constipation. Regular use of herbal ginger creams can promote a healthy gut and regular bowel movements.
remedy_properties: Digestive enzymes: Ginger contains digestive enzymes like zingibain, which helps to break down proteins and fats, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting regular bowel movements.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation in the gut lining, allowing for smoother muscle contractions and easier passage of stool.

Spasmolytic properties: The volatile oils present in ginger, such as citral and geraniol, have spasmolytic effects, which can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate constipation symptoms like bloating and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginger plant are rich in bioactive compounds that stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of constipation.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, contain a high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have natural laxative properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots and chop them into small pieces for later use.
Combine 100g of chopped ginger with 200ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until it reaches 60°c.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth to remove the ginger solids and discard them.
Allow the infused oil to cool and then whip it with 50g of beeswax and 20ml of vitamin e oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Psyllium creams helps with constipation because they work as a natural bulking agent, drawing water into the stool to soften and expand it. This makes it easier to pass through the digestive system, reducing straining and discomfort. Additionally, psyllium helps regulate bowel habits by promoting regular bowel movements and improving the movement of food through the intestines, allowing for more effective elimination of waste products.
remedy_properties: Fiber content: Psyllium cream contains a significant amount of fiber, which helps to add bulk to stool and promote regular bowel movements, reducing the likelihood of constipation.

Glycemic control: Herbal psyllium creams have been shown to slow down the absorption of sugar into the bloodstream, regulating blood sugar levels and improving insulin sensitivity, which can help alleviate constipation symptoms.

Prebiotic properties: The prebiotic fibers in psyllium cream feed beneficial gut bacteria, promoting a healthy gut microbiome, which is essential for proper digestion and bowel function, reducing the risk of constipation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soften stool and promote regular bowel movements.
Seeds: Psyllium seeds contain a type of soluble fiber that swells in water, helping to regulate bowel function and alleviate symptoms of constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried psyllium husk from plantago ovata seeds at a dosage of 10 grams per preparation.
Combine the psyllium husk with distilled water in a heat-resistant mixing bowl, stirring well to avoid lumps, for 30 minutes.
Add vaseline petroleum jelly and beeswax to the mixture, heating it gently in a double boiler over low heat, for 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping with an electric mixer until it becomes smooth and creamy, taking approximately 5 minutes.
Transfer the cream into sterile glass jars or containers, storing them in a cool dark place at a room temperature of 20°c to maintain freshness for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with constipation because they contain compounds that stimulate digestive enzymes, increasing bowel movement and relaxation of the muscles in the digestive tract. Glycyrrhizin, a key component in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the gut lining, reducing inflammation and promoting regular bowel movements. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties help to soften stool, making it easier to pass, while its antimicrobial properties prevent the overgrowth of harmful bacteria that can contribute to constipation.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to soften and lubricate stool, making it easier to pass through the digestive tract, by reducing inflammation in the gut and promoting healthy gut bacteria.

Saponins: Stimulates the movement of food through the digestive system, increasing bowel contractions and regularity, and also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and discomfort associated with constipation.
Flavonoids: Has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy digestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soften and lubricate the intestines, making it easier to pass stool.
Rhyzomes: Licorice rhizomes have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat constipation due to their ability to stimulate the digestive system and relieve bowel irregularities.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra extract for a total weight of 100 grams.
Combine the extracted glycosides with emulsifiers and preservatives in a clean glass container.
Add 20% beeswax and 10% coconut oil to the mixture while heating it to 80°c over 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool down to room temperature while stirring occasionally for 60 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized tubes or containers and store them in a cool dry place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with constipation because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing and calming the digestive tract. The curcumin present in turmeric also stimulates the movement of food through the digestive system, reducing symptoms such as bloating and discomfort. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation in the gut, allowing for regular bowel movements and promoting a healthy gut microbiome. This natural remedy can provide relief from constipation without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to constipation by slowing down digestion and gut motility.
Fiber-rich content: Contains a type of fiber called mucilage, which helps soften stool, making it easier to pass, and also stimulates bowel movements, reducing the risk of constipation.

Antioxidant properties: Has antioxidant compounds that help protect the colon from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to constipation by damaging gut lining and disrupting normal digestion.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, which is the underground stem, is rich in curcuminoids, making it a prime component for creating medicinal creams that help alleviate symptoms of constipation.
Roots: The roots of turmeric contain essential oils and compounds that aid in digestion, making them a popular choice for crafting creams that promote regular bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried turmeric powder into fine particles using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine ground turmeric powder with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 50 milliliters of coconut oil and 25 milliliters of sweet almond oil to the mixture and stir until melted.
Continue heating the mixture for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the cream from heat, let it cool to room temperature, then transfer it to an airtight container and store in the refrigerator for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with constipation because of its unique ability to soothe and lubricate the digestive tract. The mucilage present in slippery elm creates a protective barrier that reduces inflammation and irritation, allowing stool to pass through the colon more easily. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties help to soften stool, making it easier to eliminate. As a result, slippery elm creams can effectively alleviate symptoms of constipation, such as straining, discomfort, and irregular bowel movements.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm's mucilage properties create a soothing, protective barrier on the digestive tract lining, which helps to reduce inflammation and irritation associated with constipation.
Fiber content: Slippery elm contains fiber, which can help to add bulk to stool, soften it, and facilitate its passage through the digestive system, alleviating symptoms of constipation.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The herbal slippery elm cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to reduce inflammation in the colon, which can contribute to constipation, and promote a healthy gut environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their rich content of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps to soothe and lubricate the digestive tract, easing constipation symptoms.

Barks: The barks contain a high concentration of tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and irritation in the gastrointestinal tract caused by constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry ulmus rubra bark, measuring 10 grams for future use.
Combine dried ulmus rubra bark with beeswax and sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler until the beeswax is fully melted, stirring occasionally.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken, then whip it into a creamy texture using an electric mixer.
Transfer the cream to sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with constipation because they contain a combination of soothing compounds that work together to ease digestive discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties in fennel help to reduce swelling and irritation in the colon, while its natural fiber content stimulates bowel movements and relieves cramping and bloating. Additionally, the oils in fennel have been shown to relax the muscles in the intestinal tract, allowing for smoother digestion and regularity. remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel cream helps to release trapped gas in the digestive tract, easing bloating and discomfort associated with constipation.

Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to constipation by slowing down bowel movement and gut motility.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant are rich in fiber and mucilage, making them effective at soothing and lubricating the digestive tract to relieve constipation.
Barks: Fennel bark contains a natural expectorant that can help stimulate bowel movements and ease digestion, making it an effective ingredient in medicinal creams for constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried foeniculum vulgare flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground foeniculum vulgare with 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 10 drops of sweet almond oil and 5 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Continue heating the mixture for an additional 15 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 120°f to 130°f.
Remove from heat and let cool to around 100°f before pouring into small glass jars and storing in the refrigerator for up to 2 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with constipation because of its natural soothing properties that calm the digestive system. The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling in the colon, allowing for smoother bowel movements. Additionally, chamomile's relaxing effects on the muscles in the intestines help to stimulate peristalsis, which moves stool through the digestive system more efficiently. This combination of benefits helps to alleviate constipation symptoms, promoting regular and comfortable bowel movements.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to chronic constipation.

Softenening and lubricating property: The saponins and glycosides present in chamomile cream help soften stool and increase its moisture content, making it easier to pass through the digestive system.

Anxiolytic and relaxing property: Chamomile's anxiolytic compounds, such as apigenin, can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for constipation, promoting a relaxed bowel movement.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are used in medicinal creams for constipation due to their soothing and calming properties, which can help relieve digestive discomfort and promote regular bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in early morning for optimal potency and quality.
Combine 10 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 20 grams of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Infuse the mixture for 4-6 hours under low heat or overnight at room temperature to extract the herbal properties.
Strain the infusion using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids retaining only the liquid extract.
Mix the extracted liquid with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 3:1:0.5 for a final product of matricaria chamomilla cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with constipation because of its unique composition of mucilages, which create a soothing and protective barrier on the digestive tract. These mucilages help to soften stool, reduce inflammation, and promote regular bowel movements by calming the muscles in the colon and increasing gut motility. Anti-inflammatory properties: By reducing inflammation in the gut, Maidenhair Tree cream helps to soothe and calm the digestive tract, which can help to alleviate symptoms of constipation such as bloating and discomfort.

Laxative properties: The saponins present in Maidenhair Tree cream have a gentle laxative effect, helping to soften stool and promote regular bowel movements, making it easier to pass stool and relieve constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to stimulate bowel movement and relieve constipation symptoms.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and tannins, these compounds help to soften stool and increase peristalsis.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 20g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves, 100g of coconut oil and beeswax.
Combine the dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 20 minutes stirring occasionally to release the active compounds from the herbs.
Allow the mixture to cool slightly before adding 50g of melted coconut oil and 10g of melted beeswax, then stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the final mixture into a clean glass container, label and store in a dark place at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with constipation because they contain natural ingredients that stimulate digestive processes, soften stool, and reduce inflammation in the colon. The anti-inflammatory properties of elder extracts help to soothe the digestive tract, reducing discomfort and pain associated with constipation. Additionally, the demulcent properties of the cream help to protect the mucous membranes in the rectum and intestines, promoting a healthy gut environment that promotes regular bowel movements. remedy_properties: Sennosides: Sennosides stimulate bowel movements by increasing stool frequency and softening stool consistency, which helps to relieve symptoms of constipation.
Tannins: Tannins have astringent properties that help to dry up excess mucus in the gut, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with constipation.

Volatile Oils: Volatile oils contained in elder cream, such as beta-caryophyllene and limonene, have anti-inflammatory and relaxing effects on the digestive system, which can help to alleviate symptoms of constipation by soothing irritated tissues and improving gut motility.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help stimulate digestion and relieve constipation symptoms.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and protect the digestive tract, alleviating constipation discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves according to the desired yield and quality standards.
Combine 20 grams of chopped sambucus nigra flowers and leaves with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a dark glass container.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks, shaking the container daily, until the herbs infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Mix the filtered oil with 20 grams of beeswax pellets and 10 milliliters of coconut oil, melting the mixture in a double boiler under low heat for 30-45 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage creams helps with constipation because they contain soothing and calming properties that help to relax the digestive system. The anti-inflammatory compounds in borage, such as gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), reduce inflammation in the intestines, which can contribute to constipation. Additionally, borage's emollient and lubricating effects help to soften stool and make it easier to pass, reducing discomfort and promoting regular bowel movements. As a result, borage creams can provide natural relief from constipation and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and soothing effects: Borage cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like omega-6 fatty acids, which can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and soothe irritated bowels, allowing for easier passage of stool.

Stimulating and lubricating properties: The natural oils and waxes present in borage cream have a gentle stimulating effect on the digestive system, helping to increase peristalsis (intestinal contractions) and reduce constipation, while also providing a soothing and protective barrier for the mucous membranes.

Antioxidant and anti-irritant activities: Borage cream's antioxidant properties can help protect the gut lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to constipation, and its anti-irritant effects can reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soften and lubricate stool, making it easier to pass.
Seeds: Containing omega-6 fatty acids, which can help to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh borago officinalis leaves and flowers from the wild or your garden.
Dry the harvested herbs in a cool dark place for at least 3 days to reduce moisture content.
Blend 1 cup of dried herbs with 1/4 cup of sweet almond oil in a slow cooker for 6 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and combine 10ml of the herbal oil with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax melts and emulsifies, then pour into glass jars and cool to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with constipation because of its soothing properties that ease digestion and relaxation of the muscles in the intestinal tract. The menthol present in peppermint oil stimulates the contraction and movement of the digestive system, helping to move stool through the body more efficiently. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation and irritation in the intestines, further aiding in the regular passage of bowel movements. This natural remedy provides relief from constipation symptoms, promoting a healthier and more comfortable digestive experience.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and increasing the frequency of bowel movements. This allows for easier passage of stool and alleviates symptoms of constipation.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint creams, such as menthone and limonene, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to constipation.

Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol, another key component of peppermint creams, has natural lubricating properties that help to soften stool, making it easier to pass and reducing straining during bowel movements.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other volatile oils, peppermint roots help stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of constipation.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of peppermint plants contain a high concentration of menthol, which can help soothe and relax the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in the morning when the oil content is highest for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves by spreading them thinly on paper towels and letting them air dry for 24 hours.
Combine 100g of dried peppermint leaves with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil and infuse the mixture in a warm dark place for 2 weeks.

Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids then add 20% beeswax and melt the mixture over low heat until it reaches a temperature of 120°f.
Pour the melted cream base into sterilized jars and label them with the date and contents then store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with constipation because it contains isoflavones, which have natural laxative properties that stimulate bowel movement. The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds also soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with constipation. Additionally, red clover's antioxidants help repair and maintain the integrity of the gut lining, promoting a healthy gut microbiome. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal red clover creams provide natural relief from chronic constipation, helping to restore regular bowel movements and overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Isoflavones, specifically biochanin A, have been shown to increase intestinal motility and relaxation, helping to alleviate constipation by promoting regular bowel movements.

Saponins: Saponins in red clover cream may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to constipation, and also have laxative properties that aid in stool passage.

Phytoestrogens: Phytoestrogens, such as genistein, may help regulate bowel function by mimicking the effects of estrogen on the gut, promoting regular bowel movements and reducing symptoms of constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to soften stool and stimulate bowel movements.
Seeds: Contain a high amount of mucilage, a natural laxative that helps to soothe and lubricate the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality trifolium pratense leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated containing 20g of dried material.
Combine dried trifolium pratense leaves with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days allowing for maceration and infusion to occur slowly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids completely.
Mix 50ml of the herbal oil extract with 20g of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil heating gently until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curly dock creams helps with constipation because it contains saponins, a type of phytochemical that stimulates bowel movements and improves digestion. The cream also has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the gut lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with constipation. Additionally, curly dock's natural fibers help to bulk up stool, making it easier to pass and preventing straining during bowel movements. This natural remedy can provide relief from chronic constipation without relying on harsh medications.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinones: These compounds stimulate bowel movements, increase stool frequency, and soften stool texture to alleviate constipation symptoms.
Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone derivative acts as a natural laxative, increasing intestinal motility and reducing spasms in the digestive tract to promote regular bowel movements.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the gut lining and reduce inflammation associated with constipation, making it easier for stool to pass through the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of curly dock contain anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and help relieve constipation.
Rhyzomes: Curly dock rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of constipation.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried rumex crispus leaves and stems and combine them with enough water to create a strong infusion.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Mix equal parts of the infused liquid with beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10 minutes or until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency and cools slightly.
Transfer the cream to airtight containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with constipation because of its natural ability to stimulate digestive enzymes and relieve spasms in the colon. The cream's active compound, thujone, helps to increase gut motility, allowing for regular and easy bowel movements. Additionally, wormwood has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated tissues in the digestive tract, reducing discomfort and pain associated with constipation. As a result, herbal wormwood creams can provide effective relief from constipation symptoms, promoting a healthy and regular bowel routine.
remedy_properties: Irritant effects: Wormwood's bitter compounds stimulate the digestive tract, increasing contractions and movement to help relieve constipation by promoting regular bowel movements.
Antispasmodic properties: Wormwood's flavonoids and terpenes help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing muscle spasms and cramping that can contribute to constipation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Wormwood's polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote a healthy gut environment that supports regular bowel function.

Barks: The barks of wormwood contain bioactive compounds like thujone and camphor, which help to increase gut motility and alleviate constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality artemisia absinthium leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 70°f for 24 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried artemisia absinthium into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered herb with 8 tablespoons of cold-pressed coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler, heating until melted and well combined over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat, let cool to around 140°f, then whip the mixture with a hand mixer or whisk for about 5 minutes until it thickens into a smooth cream, ready for use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng creams helps with constipation because of its unique blend of herbs that stimulate digestive function, improve gut motility, and relax the muscles in the colon. The cream's ingredients work together to increase peristalsis, reducing symptoms such as bloating and discomfort associated with irregular bowel movements. Additionally, Siberian ginseng has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the digestive tract, making it easier for the body to eliminate waste regularly.
remedy_properties: Saponin content: Siberian Ginseng contains saponins, which have been shown to stimulate bowel movements and improve stool quality by increasing the frequency and consistency of bowel movements.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in Siberian Ginseng cream help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing the risk of constipation and promoting regular bowel function.

Adaptogenic activity: Siberian Ginseng is known for its adaptogenic properties, which enable it to help the body adapt to stress and promote balance in physiological processes, including digestion and bowel function, thereby alleviating symptoms of constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, the roots are used to stimulate bowel movements and relieve symptoms of constipation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including polysaccharides and polyphenols, which help to regulate gut motility and alleviate constipation.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 20 grams of dried eleutherococcus senticosus root powder into a clean glass bowl.
Combine the powder with 40 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes to melt the beeswax and mix thoroughly with an immersion blender.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before adding 20 drops of lavender essential oil and 15 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Stir well and pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars filling them about 3/4 full.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for constipation are topical preparations infused with natural herbs and plant extracts that provide relief from constipation symptoms. These creams work by stimulating bowel movements, softening stool, and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, making it easier to pass stool. Examples of herbal creams that help with constipation include Aloe vera, Slippery elm, and Senna extracts. By using these creams, individuals can experience improved digestion, reduced bloating, and a sense of relief from chronic constipation, allowing them to live a more comfortable and normal life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat constipation with herbal creams is to combine them with a balanced diet rich in fiber and fluids. Look for creams containing soothing herbs like aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula, which help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation of the digestive tract. Apply the cream topically to the abdomen or rectal area before bedtime to allow its active ingredients to work while you sleep.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to constipation that are treated with herbal creams are hemorrhoids, anal fissures, and piles. Herbal creams containing aloe vera, witch hazel, and chamomile can soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Other herbal remedies like calendula and arnica may help promote healing and reduce pain associated with these conditions. These natural ingredients offer a gentle and non-invasive approach to managing symptoms and promoting overall digestive health.
